now before the interview you need to do

preparing for the IELTS speaking test
this is what you should expect the test
will last from 11 to 14 minutes and it
will have three different parts in the
first part you will have to answer
questions about yourself whether about
your family yourself your job your home
your neighborhood your country your
studies or your interests now this part
will take about four to five minutes in
the second part you will be given a cue
card
you will have one minute to think about
what you want to say and to take some
notes and then you will have to talk for
about one to two minutes now pay
attention IDP recommends talking for the
entire two minute period until the
examiner stops you but anywhere between
one and a half minute to two minutes is
acceptable just make sure you finish all
the key points and you cover everything
before you hit the two minute limit
otherwise you will miss the points now
after you finish your two minute talk
The Examiner will ask you one or two
follow-up questions about what you just
said then you will move to part three
where you will be asked more abstract
questions which are linked to the topic
of part two this part will also last
about four to five minutes now the
assessment of your speaking skill is

coherence your pronunciation your
lexical resources or let's but it's

range and accuracy however if you are

this role and what should we consider

will have two options either to guess

if you try to guess the meaning of that

high chance of being inaccurate this
will show that you are either a bad

limited on the other hand if you ask for
clarification this is what will happen
The Examiner or the interviewer will

here's a tricky Point some students are

think it's possible to improve your
English significantly in the period of
six to seven days no well you can't have
minor improvements but you can not
improve it dramatically so what can you
do if you are given six to seven days to
prepare for an interview you have to
take Three Steps step one is to make a
list of common questions now you can go
online go on Google and then search for
the most common questions regarding that
particular interview you are going to
have for example if it's a job interview
or a University admission interview or
any other kinds of interviews step two
after you make the list what you need to
do is to write down very good answers to
those questions and step three memorize
your answers and practice practice and
practice now you might think that
memorizing is not a good option well pay
attention you have a limitation you only
have six days what else can you do so
your best chance of preparing for an
interview in six to seven days is by
memorizing some good answers now if
during the interview you are asked the
exact question you're lucky because you
have already memorize the answer for it
however if you are asked a different
question here's where it gets tricky so
you will have to use the answers you
have memorized to come up with a new
answer you will try to mix and match the
things you have memorized and that will
definitely help you in short time
however the best option overall is that
if you have time work on your general
